KNOWN FOLLOW-UPS (not blocking — documented in IMPLEMENTATION_MAP +
                  lessons.md):

  1. 🟡 requireRouteAuth() does not honor impersonation cookie.
     Files: apps/web/src/server/lib/route-auth.ts (helper),
            apps/web/src/app/api/stream/notifications/route.ts (SSE),
            apps/web/src/app/api/exports/*/route.ts (all CSV/PDF exports).
     Symptom: 4× 401s on /api/stream/notifications during impersonation
     (super_admin's raw session.user.tenantId is '' so helper rejects).
     Fix pattern: extend requireRouteAuth() to read mg-impersonate-tenant
     cookie via parseImpersonationCookieFromHeader from
     apps/web/src/lib/auth/impersonation.ts, mirror the
     `effectiveTenantId` logic from `protectedProcedure` in
     apps/web/src/server/trpc/trpc.ts (super_admin role + sessionTenantId
     === '' + cookie present → override). Required before declaring §210
     Item 4 fully closed IF impersonating super_admin needs SSE
     notifications or CSV/PDF exports. Tier 1 follow-up (~50L diff).

  2. 🟢 Banner not mounted on /admin/* routes. Currently only in
     (dashboard)/layout. UX gap when super_admin on /admin/tenants
     while impersonating — no visual reminder of current tenant. Minor
     UX polish, low priority.

  3. 🟢 Audit-while-impersonating: super_admin's actions on tenant
     resources during impersonation logged with userId=super_admin and
     tenantId=impersonatedTenantId via L5 audit. Forensic trail intact
     but does not flag "this action was performed by an impersonating
     platform admin". Adding an `impersonatedBy` field to audit_logs or
     augmenting audit writes with a context flag would close the gap.
     NOT in §210 scope.

DEV WORKFLOW NOTE (locked 2026-05-30):
  Dev compose uses production multi-stage Dockerfile — NO source mount.
  Source changes do NOT reach the container until rebuild + recreate.
  Standard QA pre-flight after a feature ship:
    docker compose -f deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml build app
    cd deploy/compose/dev
    set -a; source ../../../.env.dev; set +a
    docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d --no-build --force-recreate app
  Container Ready logs appear in ~200ms after recreate. Then navigate
  to http://localhost:45204 for QA.
